result:
Pass complete. All changes validated and the progress log updated. (Note: `components/search-dialog.tsx` showing as untracked is pre-existing from an earlier pass — the static search is already wired; not part of my changes. I did not commit, per the pass instructions.)

SUMMARY:
Added a standardized **"De un vistazo"** orientation header to all 16 AESIA guides, closing the navigation loop opened by Passes 1–2 (`start` → `itinerarios` → guide → next steps). Created one reusable server component, `components/guia-resumen.tsx` (registered in `components/mdx.tsx`), rendering an accessible `<aside>`/`<dl>` card with native Fumadocs tokens. Each guide now shows, at a glance: the **Reglamento article** it develops, its **layer** (Conocer/Implementar/Autodiagnosticar), **who is responsible** (operator role), **when to use it**, **related guides** (linked chips), and a fixed link back to its **itinerario por perfil**. Files changed: `components/guia-resumen.tsx` (new, 100 lines), `components/mdx.tsx`, the 16 `content/docs/guides/*.mdx`, and `.hermes/overnight/progress.md`. Article↔guide mappings were cross-checked against both `summary.mdx` and each guide's own transcribed text (e.g., 03→Art. 43, 17/9/14/10/13/15/12/72/73, 15→Art. 11+Anexo IV) to avoid introducing unverified data; links use `next/link` so GitHub Pages' `basePath` is applied automatically.

VALIDATION:
`npm run build` succeeded — TypeScript passes (no `ignoreBuildErrors`), 75 static pages generated, 0 errors. Verified in the exported HTML (`out/docs/guides/*/index.html`) that the `<aside>` renders with its `dl/dt/dd` structure, capa tag, the `·` role separator, related-guide chips and the itinerario link; all 16 guides emit the itinerario link, and internal hrefs resolve to real generated pages with correct trailing slashes (`/docs/guides/12-guia-de-registros/`, `/docs/itinerarios/`) — no broken links. Could not visually screenshot in-browser, but static markup and styling classes are confirmed correct.

NEXT_IDEA:
The per-guide metadata now lives as inline MDX props. Next, centralize it into a single typed source (guide frontmatter or `lib/guides-meta.ts`) and build a **`/docs/guides` index page** presenting the 16 guides as a scannable, comparable **matrix** (number, title, article, layer, role, when) instead of only the linear sidebar — plus an article-number badge in the nav. This removes the duplication introduced this pass and adds a "choose your guide" overview that doesn't exist today.
